results.title: ToolSense: Details, Reviews, Pricing, & Features | CheckThat.ai
results.content:
Users praise asset tracking capabilities, real-time data access, customizable forms, and mobile functionality. Documented deployments span construction asset management, maintenance scheduling, preventive maintenance workflows, and IoT integration scenarios.

G2 and TrustRadius list the product but show zero user reviews—approximately 20% coverage among major B2B software review platforms examined.

The single-platform concentration prevents cross-validation of feedback patterns and identification of common pain points. No specific complaints or negative feedback surfaced from any reviewed platform, though this may reflect insufficient feedback volume rather than absence of limitations. [...] Multi-industry versatility across construction, manufacturing, facility management, hospitality, and agriculture contrasts with vertical-specific competitors. The accessibility-first design emphasizes field operations and user adoption—addressing common resistance to enterprise asset management systems.

Total cost of ownership focus through integrated parts management, lifecycle tracking, and proactive scheduling differentiates from competitors emphasizing hardware capabilities over operational workflow integration.

## ToolSense User Sentiment

ToolSense maintains concentrated review presence with 67 reviews on Capterra averaging 4.7 out of 5 stars. The platform scores 4.5/5 for both ease of use and customer support. [...] ## ToolSense Company Overview

ToolSense launched in 2017 from Austria to replace spreadsheets and paperwork with centralized digital asset tracking. CEO Alexander Manafi leads the company, which has raised $12 million across multiple funding rounds—most recently an €8 million Series A in November 2025 led by PwC's Industrial Internet of Things venture arm.

The platform addresses equipment downtime, manual process elimination, and compliance management across asset-intensive operations. Seven years in market with sustained venture backing signals operational stability beyond typical startup volatility.

### What do users say about ToolSense?

Reviewers indicate ToolSense offers an intuitive and user-friendly interface, making it easy for them to manage tasks, track resources, and coordinate teams. They appreciate the seamless navigation, clear module labeling, and the ability to streamline multiple tools into one platform, which saves time and reduces frustration. [...] Some say the customization options are limited and would like more features, especially for workflow and notification settings. They find the mobile app less feature-rich than the web version and mention occasional navigation issues. They also note that integration with ERP systems is missing and onboarding can feel overwhelming initially.

Navigation

  

Among reviewers who comment on ToolSense's navigation, 81% feel positive. Users say the interface is intuitive, user-friendly, and helps streamline tasks. They find it easy to locate features and appreciate clear organization. However, they mention limited customization options and occasional navigation issues, especially on the website and mobile app. [...] Value for money rating:

results.title: Honest ToolSense Review: Is ToolSense a Good CMMS? - Coast
results.content:
In short, ToolSense saves time, cuts downtime and keeps everything organized — all in one user-friendly app. Read this ToolSense review to get a better idea of whether or not this CMMS solution will work for you.

29

out of 35

Ease of Use

4

Mobile Experience

5

Core Features & Functionality

4

Adaptability & Customization

4

Customer Support

4

Pricing

4

Current Reviews

4

#### 

#### Pros

#### Cons

We reviewed ToolSense using a three-pronged approach: internet research, ToolSense’s own website and user reviews across G2, Capterra, Reddit and forums. Here’s what we found.

Trust Icon [...] In online reviews, customer support receives mostly positive feedback. Users often describe the support team as responsive, knowledgeable and eager to assist. That said, a few users have noted slower-than-expected replies and occasional difficulty reaching someone by phone or chat. 

Overall, ToolSense’s support is a strong point, especially for teams that rely on clear guidance during setup and day-to-day use.

## ToolSense Pricing [...] Features like asset creation, work order generation and location setup are easy to find and use. With just a few clicks, users can log maintenance issues, assign work orders and link assets to specific sites. The drag-and-drop functionality and logical layout make navigating through the system feel smooth. Updating a work order — whether changing status, adding notes or attaching images—is quick and can be done on both desktop and mobile. Searching for existing work orders is equally efficient, with filters for status, priority, date, technician and location making it simple to locate exactly what you need. 

Overall, ToolSense strikes a solid balance between simplicity and depth, making it accessible for everyday users while still offering the functionality maintenance teams expect.

results.title: What Is ToolSense? Learn About Advantages and Use Cases | ToolSense
results.content:
### Supervisor / Site Manager / Machine Manager

Once a site manager or supervisor has equipped a machine with a QR code, their job becomes much easier thanks to ToolSense, leaving them more time for important tasks. With ToolSense, they are given an easy-to-use interface for receiving tickets about machine problems and to-dos which have been filed by machine operators. ToolSense can be used to plan equipment checks and review a machine’s runtime. If a site manager is authorised to, they can easily request new machines, machine transports, or the disposal of broken equipment thanks to ToolSense.

Supervisor / Site Manager / Machine Manager: ToolSense interface

### Purchasing / Management [...] ### Machine Operator

If a device, machine or vehicle malfunctions, there is not much the machine operator can do about it. In a strict chain of command, the employee would have no access to tech support and go through a string of supervisors to report a malfunction. With ToolSense, all a machine operator needs is a device capable of scanning the QR code that is placed on the machine. That way, they can easily report an issue or request necessary consumables. Their query automatically appears in the system as a ticket and can be solved by a machine manager.

### Supervisor / Site Manager / Machine Manager
